message:
test(changelog): escape backslash-dollar in grep patterns so the heuristic-detection test actually matches the prohibited pattern

The `test_runtime_helpers_do_not_guess_home_paths_from_usernames`
guard inside test_changelog_export_status.sh greps the lib files
to verify none of them re-introduce the
  printf '/home/%s\n' "$current_user"
heuristic that the prior fail-closed-target-home commits removed.

The grep pattern was being assembled with `"\$current_user"` and
`"\$TARGET_USER"` — but in this bash heredoc-style context the
single backslash gets eaten by the outer interpolation before grep
sees it, so the pattern was actually searching for the literal
string `printf '/home/%s\n' "current_user"` (no `$`), which never
matches anything.  As a result, the regression guard was a no-op:
removing the heuristic produced "no matches" (correct), but
re-introducing it would also produce "no matches" (silently
passing).

Escaping the dollar twice (`"\\\$current_user"`,
`"\\\$TARGET_USER"`) propagates a single literal `\$` through the
outer interpolation into grep, where `\$` matches a literal `$` in
the file content — so the pattern now matches the prohibited form
the test claims to detect.

Also adds a `harness_section` line for the Info/Support/Onboard
group of tests so the test output groups the recent additions
under the right header instead of dumping them under the prior
section.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -6608,15 +6608,15 @@ test_runtime_helpers_do_not_guess_home_paths_from_usernames() {
     local output=""
 
     output="$( {
-        grep -RFn "printf '/home/%s\\n' \"\\$current_user\"" \
+        grep -RFn "printf '/home/%s\\n' \"\\\$current_user\"" \
             "$CONTINUE_SH" "$DASHBOARD_SH" "$INFO_SH" "$CHANGELOG_SH" "$EXPORT_CONFIG_SH" \
             "$STATUS_SH" "$SUPPORT_SH" "$CHEATSHEET_SH" "$DOCTOR_SH" \
             "$REPO_ROOT/scripts/lib/doctor_fix.sh" "$NOTIFY_SH" "$NOTIFICATIONS_SH" \
             "$WEBHOOK_SH" "$SMOKE_TEST_SH" "$STATE_SH" 2>/dev/null || true
         grep -RFn 'echo "/home/$user"' \
             "$CONTINUE_SH" "$DASHBOARD_SH" "$INFO_SH" "$CHANGELOG_SH" "$EXPORT_CONFIG_SH" \
             "$STATUS_SH" "$SUPPORT_SH" "$CHEATSHEET_SH" 2>/dev/null || true
-        grep -RFn "printf '/home/%s\\n' \"\\$TARGET_USER\"" "$STATE_SH" 2>/dev/null || true
+        grep -RFn "printf '/home/%s\\n' \"\\\$TARGET_USER\"" "$STATE_SH" 2>/dev/null || true
         grep -RFn 'TARGET_HOME="/home/$TARGET_USER"' "$SMOKE_TEST_SH" 2>/dev/null || true
     } )"
 
@@ -6783,7 +6783,9 @@ main() {
 
     harness_section "Info / Support / Onboard"
     test_state_driven_helpers_reject_invalid_target_home_from_state || true
+    test_runtime_helpers_do_not_guess_home_paths_from_usernames || true
     test_runtime_helpers_resolve_current_home_from_passwd_when_home_invalid || true
+    test_runtime_helpers_fail_closed_when_current_home_unresolved || true
     test_runtime_helpers_fail_closed_on_invalid_passwd_home_for_target_user || true
     test_info_uses_installed_layout_under_root_home || true
     test_info_uses_system_state_target_home_when_getent_unavailable || true
